---
title: "Template Variables"
---

DAG files support Go `text/template` expressions, expanded at run time.

## Available variables

| Variable | Example value | Description |
|----------|---------------|-------------|
| `{{ .Today }}` | `2026-04-05` | Current date (YYYY-MM-DD) |
| `{{ .Now }}` | `2026-04-05T08:30:00Z` | Current time (RFC3339) |
| `{{ .Params.name }}` | `sales` | Parameter value (from defaults or `-p` overrides) |
| `{{ .Env.KEY }}` | `localhost` | DAG-level environment variable |
| `{{ .Matrix.key }}` | `lm` | Matrix parameter (in matrix-expanded steps) |

## Usage examples

In environment variables:

```yaml
env:
  REPORT_DATE: "{{ .Today }}"
  LOG_FILE: "logs/run_{{ .Now }}.log"
```

In step arguments:

```yaml
- id: extract
  script: etl/extract.R
  args: ["--dept", "{{ .Params.department }}", "--date", "{{ .Today }}"]
```

In step fields:

```yaml
- id: fit
  script: models/fit.R
  args: ["--algo", "{{ .Matrix.algo }}"]
  matrix:
    algo: [lm, glm, gam]
```

## Template syntax

daggle uses Go's `text/template` package. A few useful patterns:

```yaml
# Conditional default
env:
  OUTPUT: '{{ if .Params.output }}{{ .Params.output }}{{ else }}results.csv{{ end }}'
```

Template expressions are expanded before YAML parsing, so they work in any string field.
